name: write_or_update_file
description_lines: Write a new file, completely rewrite an existing file, or update specific sections of a file.
Use mode=write to create/overwrite entire files.
Use mode=update to replace specific code sections in existing files.
Modifying .gd or .gdshader files automatically runs diagnostics on the edited file and appends syntax/type/reference or shader parser diagnostics to the result.

Successful C# writes deliberately defer diagnostics so related multi-file edits can be completed before validation. Their results set validation_required=true and recommend one script_diagnostics call with scan_project:true after the related C# edits are finished.
A C# write never waits for or cancels the plugin's initial background build. If it overlaps that build, the next explicit project scan performs one forced refresh.
Do not run C# diagnostics or a build after every C# write. Use targeted C# script_diagnostics only when a focused explicit check is needed.
Modifying a .gdshader also scans .tscn and .tres files that reference that shader and reserializes those owners through Godot so stale embedded ShaderMaterial data is rewritten.
The result includes reserialized_resources, reserialize_warnings, and reserialize_skipped.
Blocked paths: res://project.godot, res://.godot/, res://.git/, res://addons/fennara/, and any plugin.cfg.
Only write to user project files.

mode.description: Operation mode: 'write' to create or completely rewrite a file with new content, 'update' to replace specific sections in an existing file

file_path.description: Path to the file relative to project root (e.g., 'scripts/enemy.gd') or absolute path (e.g., 'res://scripts/enemy.gd')

content.description: WRITE MODE ONLY: Full content of the file to write. Required when mode='write'.

old_string.description: UPDATE MODE ONLY: Exact code to find and replace. Must match exactly including whitespace and indentation. Required when mode='update'. If this appears multiple times in the file, include more surrounding code to make it unique.

new_string.description: UPDATE MODE ONLY: Code to insert in place of old_string. Can be empty string to delete code. Required when mode='update'.

replace_all.description: UPDATE MODE ONLY: Whether to replace all occurrences of old_string (default: false). If false and multiple matches exist, tool will return an error asking for more context.

attach_to_scene.description: WRITE MODE ONLY: Optional. Scene file path to attach this script to (e.g., 'scenes/player.tscn'). Only works for .gd scripts. Requires attach_to_node to also be specified.

attach_to_node.description: WRITE MODE ONLY: Optional. Node path within the scene to attach script to (e.g., 'Player', 'Enemy', '.' for root). Use get_node() syntax - do NOT include the scene root node name in the path unless targeting a child. Required if attach_to_scene is provided.
